source: User-supplied competition build brief; no additional Phase-0 interview was required.
goal: Build, verify, publish, and present Codex Control Tower as a competition-ready local-first governance layer for Codex-assisted software delivery.
audience: OpenAI Build Week judges, developers using Codex, AI-agent workflow builders, and open-source reviewers.
successCriteria: A working CLI and dashboard, a clear 25-to-88 simulated before/after demo, honest evidence boundaries, protected source lineage, passing local verification, and a reviewable public repository.
forbiddenAreas: Do not modify the four protected source repositories, commit secrets or private screenshots, claim simulated evidence as real, add mandatory cloud dependencies, or rewrite Git history.
risk: The largest immediate risk is a trust product that does not govern or truthfully assess its own repository; competition eligibility and Codex-use provenance must also remain explicit.
expectedEvidence: Full test suite, deterministic demo output, dashboard production build, root self-scan, source-protection checks, clean staged diff, remote commit verification, and visible NOT_RUN gates.
nextMission: Fix root self-scan false positives, complete real repository governance records, rerun full verification, then publish the approved main branch to the user-provided GitHub repository.
